faqs.a: Toxic positivity is the pressure to stay upbeat at all times and to dismiss difficult emotions with a positive spin. It is a popular term, not a clinical diagnosis, and the problem is using positivity to silence real feelings rather than optimism itself.

faqs.q: How is toxic positivity different from optimism?
faqs.a: Healthy optimism leaves room for the full range of emotions and says this is hard and we will get through it. Toxic positivity dismisses the hard feeling entirely and insists that only the bright side is allowed.

When painful feelings get treated as failures of attitude, people learn to hide them, which tends to make distress harder to process. On the receiving end it can feel dismissive, and aimed inward it can create guilt for not feeling okay.
